home.title: Build the ecosystem first. Package it for each customer second.
home.body: One operating platform for hatcheries, farms, and research teams that connects human records, board data, monitoring, and the path to automation.
home.caption: Start with field-ready operations, then grow into control and orchestration without rebuilding the system story.
home.panelTitle: The first operating layer for Daily Complete.
home.panelBody: Start with real daily work, structured records, and monitoring. Then grow into device integration, control logic, and deeper automation.

audiences.label: Research, farm operators, and private sector teams

inputs.label: Capture data from both staff and connected boards

readiness.label: Expand into automation through the roadmap

ecosystem.title: More than smart farm monitoring
ecosystem.body: The company story is not a single dashboard. It is a connected operating ecosystem for data capture, visibility, and future control.

flagship.title: Daily Complete is the first flagship solution
flagship.body: Daily Complete anchors the first real workflow surface, then expands toward deeper device integration and automation over time.

roadmap.title: From monitoring to orchestration
roadmap.body: Today the system focuses on monitoring. The long-term platform moves toward rule-based actions and multi-step process control.

home.ecosystemTitle: One architecture that accepts manual work now and automation later.
home.ecosystemBody: ArayaShiki Lab is building a platform that can serve different operating models without forcing every customer into the same level of automation on day one.

human.title: Human data capture
human.body: Daily work, research observations, and operational records stay structured from the moment someone enters them.

board.title: Board and sensor input
board.body: The same system is prepared to receive telemetry, device state, and machine-originated events from connected boards.

rules.title: Rule-based automation
rules.body: Thresholds and simple if-then actions create the first bridge from monitoring into controlled responses.

orchestration.title: Process orchestration
orchestration.body: The long-term direction is multi-step control such as water preparation, mixing, and traceable execution across devices.

home.solutionsTitle: The ecosystem is broad internally, but the market story should stay clear.
home.solutionsBody: ArayaShiki Lab can build a full capability map behind the scenes, then present the right package for each audience instead of forcing one generic product pitch.

research.title: Research & education
research.body: Support repeatable observation, structured data collection, and learning environments for institutes and academic teams.

operations.title: Farm operations
operations.body: Fit daily hatchery and farm work first, then improve visibility, monitoring, and practical operational control.

enterprise.title: Enterprise & custom automation
enterprise.body: Extend into private-sector workflows, permissions, integrations, and future process automation where standard packages are not enough.

home.roadmapTitle: The system grows in layers instead of pretending everything is already automated.
home.roadmapBody: Current delivery is monitoring-led. The roadmap expands into rule-based control and later into complex operational automation such as salinity-controlled water mixing.

monitoring.title: Monitoring
monitoring.body: SB-00 establishes the first production-ready monitoring surface and validates how records, telemetry, and visibility should work together.

automation.title: Rule-based actions
automation.body: Next layers add condition-based on and off control, alert reactions, and safer automation patterns tied to real operating rules.

process.title: Process automation
process.body: Future workflows can calculate targets such as 25 ppt water preparation, drive pumps and valves, and record the completed operation in the same platform.

home.ctaTitle: See the company as an ecosystem, then explore the first flagship solution.
home.ctaBody: Use the ecosystem story to explain the whole direction, and use Daily Complete to prove how the first operational layer works in practice.

solutionsPage.title: One ecosystem, three market-facing solution tracks.
solutionsPage.body: ArayaShiki Lab can build a broad operational foundation internally, then package the right layer for research teams, farm operators, and private-sector projects.
solutionsPage.highlightTitle: Daily Complete is the first production-facing operating surface.
solutionsPage.highlightBody: It starts with real daily work, structured records, and monitoring, then grows toward integrated control and automation as the hardware and process layers mature.
solutionsPage.tracksTitle: Different customers need different starting points, not different company stories.
solutionsPage.tracksBody: Each track highlights the same ecosystem from a different operational need. The product surface can change, but the architecture and narrative stay connected.

research.title: Research & education
research.body: Built for institutes, labs, and training environments that need structured data capture and repeatable operational records.
bullets: Manual and sensor data in one system
Usable for applied research and learning environments

operations.title: Farm operations
operations.body: Built for hatcheries and farms that need practical daily workflows before they can move into deeper automation.
bullets: Daily work logs that fit real operations
Monitoring and alerts for limited staff teams
A path from records into controlled automation

enterprise.title: Enterprise & custom automation
enterprise.body: Built for private-sector operators who need integrations, permissions, and future process control beyond a standard package.
bullets: Roadmap toward process automation and orchestration

solutionsPage.layersTitle: Every package still sits on the same operating stack.
solutionsPage.layersBody: ArayaShiki Lab is not separating the company into unrelated products. Each solution track moves through the same capability ladder at a different pace.

capture.title: Capture
capture.body: Start with reliable records from people, tasks, and operational checkpoints.

monitoring.title: Monitoring
monitoring.body: Bring in board and sensor data so teams can see state, trends, and deviations.

automation.title: Rule-based control
automation.body: Add safer if-then actions, threshold handling, and controlled responses.

orchestration.title: Process orchestration
orchestration.body: Expand into multi-step workflows such as water preparation, mixing, and traceable automation.

solutionsPage.ctaTitle: Use the ecosystem story to open the conversation, then use the right solution track to narrow it.
solutionsPage.ctaBody: This keeps the company positioning broad while making the first customer conversation concrete.

aboutPage.title: ArayaShiki Lab is building an operating ecosystem, not a single isolated tool.
aboutPage.body: We are developing a smart hatchery and aquaculture ecosystem that connects manual records, board data, monitoring, and future automation so farms, hatcheries, and research teams can grow on one architecture.
aboutPage.highlightTitle: The company story starts broader than any one pilot device or dashboard.
aboutPage.highlightBody: That is why we present ArayaShiki Lab at the company level, Daily Complete as the flagship operating layer, and SB-00 as one active monitoring path inside the ecosystem.
aboutPage.principlesTitle: The ecosystem is designed around real work first.
aboutPage.principlesBody: We are not starting with abstract automation promises. The architecture begins with practical operations and expands only when the next layer becomes trustworthy.

operations.title: Operations come first
operations.body: The system must fit daily work, not just look advanced in a demo.

connected.title: One connected stack
connected.body: Human records, device data, alerts, and future control should live in the same system story.

growth.title: Growth by layers
growth.body: Teams should be able to start with records and monitoring, then move toward automation without changing platforms.

aboutPage.audiencesTitle: Different users can enter the same ecosystem from different starting points.
aboutPage.audiencesBody: The company serves multiple audiences, but each one should still recognize the same platform foundation behind the packaging.

research.title: Research and education teams
research.body: Need repeatable records, structured observations, and systems that support applied learning and experiments.

farm.title: Farm and hatchery operators
farm.body: Need practical daily workflows, clear monitoring, and a realistic path into safer automation.

enterprise.title: Private-sector and custom projects
enterprise.body: Need permissions, integrations, and process-level control that can expand beyond a standard deployment.

aboutPage.roadmapTitle: The company roadmap moves from visibility into control.
aboutPage.roadmapBody: This is how ArayaShiki Lab can support different customers at different maturity levels without fragmenting the architecture.

capture.title: Capture
capture.body: Structure records from people, tasks, and field activity.

monitoring.title: Monitoring
monitoring.body: Connect telemetry and status from boards and sensors.

control.title: Rule-based control
control.body: Add controlled responses to clear conditions and thresholds.

process.title: Process orchestration
process.body: Expand into multi-step operational workflows such as water preparation and mixing.

aboutPage.ctaTitle: Start with the company story, then move into the solution that fits the conversation.
aboutPage.ctaBody: This keeps the positioning coherent while giving each audience a practical entry point.

packagesPage.title: Choose the right entry point without fragmenting the ecosystem.
packagesPage.body: ArayaShiki Lab builds one operating foundation, then packages the starting layer that best fits each customer. The package changes, but the system story stays connected.
packagesPage.highlightTitle: Packages are how customers enter, not separate products with separate architecture.
packagesPage.highlightBody: This keeps the company positioning clear while giving research teams, farm operators, and private-sector projects a concrete commercial starting point.
packagesPage.packagesTitle: Three starting points built on the same stack.
packagesPage.packagesBody: Each package focuses on a different operating need. Over time, customers can expand into more monitoring, control, and custom automation without replacing the platform.

research.title: Research & education package
research.body: Designed for data capture, repeatable observations, and practical learning or applied-research workflows.
deliverables: Structured manual records and experiment tracking
Sensor data visibility alongside human observations
Operational views suitable for teaching and research teams

operations.fit: For hatcheries and farms running daily work
operations.title: Farm operations package
operations.body: Designed to support daily hatchery operations first, then improve monitoring, alerts, and controlled automation as the team is ready.
deliverables: Daily work logs and operational checklists
Monitoring surfaces for device and field visibility
A phased path into safer rule-based automation

enterprise.title: Enterprise & custom automation package
enterprise.body: Designed for organizations that need permissions, integrations, and more advanced process logic beyond a standard rollout.
deliverables: Roadmap planning for boards, actuators, and workflows

packagesPage.principlesTitle: The package shapes the first conversation, not the entire roadmap.
packagesPage.principlesBody: This is how ArayaShiki Lab can stay broad as a company while still presenting a clear offer to each type of customer.

start.title: Start with the right operating problem
start.body: Lead with the work the customer is trying to stabilize first, whether that is record keeping, monitoring, or a custom process requirement.

shared.title: Keep one shared architecture
shared.body: Do not create disconnected products for each audience. Each package should remain part of the same ecosystem and data model.

expand.title: Expand by readiness, not by rewrite
expand.body: Customers should be able to move from monitoring into control and orchestration as hardware, process, and trust mature.

packagesPage.ctaTitle: Open with the company story, narrow with solutions, and close with the right package.
packagesPage.ctaBody: That sequence lets ArayaShiki Lab look credible at the ecosystem level while staying practical in customer conversations.

requestDemoPage.title: Start the conversation around your operating model, not a generic product pitch.
requestDemoPage.body: Use this page to frame the first discussion with ArayaShiki Lab around the kind of facility you run, the data you already have, and the level of monitoring or automation you want to grow into.
requestDemoPage.highlightTitle: A strong demo starts with the right use case and readiness level.
requestDemoPage.highlightBody: ArayaShiki Lab can show the same ecosystem through a research, farm-operations, or enterprise lens. The more clearly that starting point is defined, the more useful the first walkthrough becomes.
requestDemoPage.pathsTitle: Choose the conversation that matches the real work.
requestDemoPage.pathsBody: These are not different products. They are different ways to enter the same platform story depending on what your team needs first.

research.title: Research and education walkthrough
research.body: Focus the demo on structured records, repeatable observations, and the connection between manual work and sensor-supported data.

operations.title: Farm operations walkthrough
operations.body: Focus the demo on day-to-day hatchery work, monitoring, alerts, and the practical path into safer rule-based automation.

enterprise.title: Enterprise and custom automation walkthrough
enterprise.body: Focus the demo on integrations, permissions, multi-site workflows, and future process orchestration requirements.

requestDemoPage.checklistTitle: Bring enough context to make the first session concrete.
requestDemoPage.checklistBody: You do not need a full specification yet. A short operational picture is enough to make the conversation much sharper.

workflow.title: Current workflow
workflow.body: Explain the daily work that matters most right now, such as observations, tank checks, water preparation, or routine hatchery operations.

inputs.title: Current inputs
inputs.body: Describe what data comes from people today and what might come from sensors, boards, or machines later.

outcomes.title: Target outcomes
outcomes.body: Clarify whether the first goal is visibility, cleaner records, alerts, operator support, or a future automation path.

scale.title: Deployment scope
scale.body: Share whether this starts as a learning center, one hatchery, one farm team, or a broader private-sector rollout.

requestDemoPage.formTitle: Draft the first demo brief here.
requestDemoPage.formBody: Fill in a few details and the page will prepare a concise brief you can copy or open in your email client.

form.useCasePlaceholder: Describe the operation or process you want the first demo to focus on.
form.currentInputsPlaceholder: Describe what is recorded by people today and what may come from boards or sensors later.
form.nearTermGoalsPlaceholder: For example: cleaner records, monitoring, alerts, or the first automation layer.
form.deploymentScopePlaceholder: For example: one learning center, one hatchery, or multi-site rollout.
form.previewTitle: Use this summary as the first contact message.
form.previewBody: You can copy the text below or open it directly in your email client when a contact address is configured.
form.facebookReady: You can take this brief to Facebook Messenger right away.
form.socialReady: You can use Facebook or LINE for quick contact with this prepared brief.
form.allReady: Choose the contact path that fits best: Facebook or LINE for quick conversation, or email for a more formal introduction.
form.contactReady: Choose the contact path that fits best: Facebook for quick conversation, or email for a more formal introduction.
form.emailReady: Your email client will open with the prepared brief.
form.emailMissing: Set NEXT_PUBLIC_CONTACT_EMAIL to enable direct email drafting. Until then, copy this brief and send it through your preferred channel.

requestDemoPage.templateTitle: Use this structure when preparing a demo request.
requestDemoPage.templateBody: This template helps keep the first conversation practical and aligned with the package and solution track that fits best.

organization.label: Organization type:
organization.value: Research institute, learning center, farm, hatchery, or private company

useCase.label: Primary use case:
useCase.value: What operation or process should the first demo focus on?

inputs.label: Data and device inputs:
inputs.value: What is recorded manually today, and what devices or boards may connect later?

goals.label: Near-term goals:
goals.value: Monitoring, record quality, alerts, control, or process automation

nextStep.label: Preferred next step:
nextStep.value: Intro walkthrough, package fit review, or deeper solution discussion

requestDemoPage.ctaTitle: Use packages to define the entry point, then use the demo to shape the rollout.
requestDemoPage.ctaBody: That sequence keeps the company narrative broad while making the next commercial step specific and useful.

auth.forgotPasswordBody: SB-00 uses Google, Facebook, and Apple sign-in only. Recover access from the provider account you used to log in.
auth.resetPasswordBody: Password changes are handled by Google, Facebook, or Apple because this portal does not store customer passwords.
auth.oauthOnlyBody: Use the same Google, Facebook, or Apple account every time so farm membership and device permissions stay attached to one identity.
auth.signupOauthBody: Create your portal account with Google, Facebook, or Apple. We will collect farm profile details after the first sign-in.
auth.oauthRecoveryBody: This portal is OAuth-only. If you cannot access the account, recover it directly with Google, Facebook, or Apple, then return here and sign in again.
auth.portalTitle: ArayaShiki Lab access for SB-00 farms
auth.portalBody: Sign in to monitor field devices, bind QR codes, and manage SB-00 farm permissions from a mobile-first dashboard.

dashboard.title: Farm overview
dashboard.authPending: The dashboard route is guarded, but login is deferred until NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL and NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY are configured.

placeholder.opsBody: Review fleet health, open alerts, command activity, and notification readiness from the operator console.
placeholder.deviceBody: Review latest telemetry, alert state, command history, and permitted device actions.

provision.resolveFirst: Resolve the device before choosing a farm.

farmSettings.resellersBody: External support access stays scoped and auditable.

farmSettings.inviteBody: Invite by email and choose only the access they need.
farmSettings.assignResellerBody: Grant support permissions to a known reseller user ID.

deviceDetail.noAlerts: No alerts found for this device.

ops.notAuthorized: This page is available only to admin/operator users.

settings.title: Account settings
settings.body: Choose the display name and language this browser should use across the portal.

sections.title: What we collect
body: ArayaShiki Lab uses Google, Facebook, or Apple sign-in for the SB-00 Portal to identify users. We store account profile details such as display name, email address, preferred language, role, farm membership, invite status, and audit records needed to operate the service.
For connected farms and devices, we may store farm names, device identifiers, device status, telemetry readings, alert history, command history, notification preferences, and provisioning records.

sections.title: How we use data
body: We use this data to authenticate users, restrict access with farm-scoped permissions, show device dashboards, send or record alerts, process provisioning actions, and maintain operational audit trails.
We do not sell customer farm or device data.

sections.title: Service providers
body: The portal is hosted with Vercel and uses Supabase for authentication and database services. OAuth identity providers such as Google, Facebook, and Apple process sign-in according to their own policies.
Notification providers such as email or messaging services may be used when farm owners configure alert delivery.

sections.title: Retention and access
body: Farm data is retained while the farm account or device record remains active, unless deletion is requested or required by law. Access is limited by role, farm membership, reseller assignment, and administrative audit controls.
For privacy or access requests, contact the app owner using the support email configured in the OAuth provider profile.

sections.title: Use of the portal
body: The SB-00 Portal by ArayaShiki Lab is provided to monitor farms, devices, alerts, provisioning, and operational support workflows. Users must sign in with an approved OAuth provider and may only access farms and devices they are authorized to view or manage.
Users are responsible for keeping their provider accounts secure and for using device commands carefully.

sections.title: Roles and permissions
body: Farm owners control farm membership and notification settings. Farm members, resellers, and operators receive only the permissions assigned to them. Administrative or reseller activity may be logged for audit and support purposes.

sections.title: Operational limits
body: Telemetry, alerts, and device commands depend on network connectivity, device firmware, external providers, and hardware availability. The service should not be treated as the only safety mechanism for critical farm operations until field validation and operating procedures are complete.

sections.title: Changes
body: These terms may be updated as the pilot and production service mature. Continued use of the portal after updates means the user accepts the updated terms.

sections.title: How to request deletion
body: To request deletion of SB-00 Portal account data, contact ArayaShiki Lab using the support email configured in the OAuth provider profile and include the email address used to sign in.
If you are a farm owner, specify whether you want to delete only your personal profile or also request removal of farm, device, telemetry, alert, and provisioning records under your farm.

sections.title: What happens next
body: We will verify the requester, review farm ownership and legal or operational retention requirements, then remove or anonymize eligible account and farm records.
Some audit, security, or operational records may be retained where required to protect the service, investigate incidents, or comply with applicable obligations.

sections.title: OAuth provider data
body: Deleting portal data does not delete the user account at Google, Facebook, or Apple. Manage provider-side account and app permissions directly through the provider account settings.
